author: Lee Child
passage:
A fat guy sat at a big rosewood desk. benind nim
were a couple of big flags. ‘There was a Stars and
Stripes with a gold fringe on the left and what I
guessed was the Georgia state flag on the right. On
the wall between the flags was a clock. It was a big
old round thing framed in mahogany. Looked like
it had decades of polish on it. I figured it must be
the clock from whatever old station house they bull-
dozed to build this new place. I figured the
architect had used it to give a sense of history to
the new building. It was showing nearly twelve-
thirty.

ambiguities: The identity of the fat guy
scene_brief.image_prompt: A spacious office featuring a large, polished rosewood desk at the center. Behind the desk, two prominent flags stand tall: one is the Stars and Stripes adorned with a gold fringe, and the other is the Georgia state flag, vibrant and proud. On the wall between the flags, an antique round clock, framed in rich mahogany, ticks away the time, its surface gleaming with decades of polish. The atmosphere is one of authority and history, with the clock indicating nearly twelve-thirty.

author: Lee Child
passage: The diner was small, but bright and clean. Brand new, built to resemble a converted railroad car. Narrow, with long lunch counter on one side and a kitchen bumped out back. Booths lining the opposite wall. A doorway where the centre booth would be.

scene_brief.setting: a small diner resembling a converted railroad car
scene_brief.image_prompt: A small, bright diner designed to look like a converted railroad car. The interior features a long, shiny lunch counter on one side, with a small kitchen area bumped out at the back. On the opposite wall, cozy booths are lined up, inviting patrons to sit and enjoy their meals. A doorway stands prominently where the center booth would be, suggesting a welcoming entrance to this clean and modern establishment.
